figures.caption: Figure 1: Comparison of cross-document attention patterns. Left: Existing approaches perform token-wise cross-attention between documents, introducing noise and distracting attention. Right: GSA first compresses each document into high-level gist tokens (g), and restricts cross-document interaction to them. Raw tokens attend locally within each document, resulting in a cleaner, more structured, and efficient attention mechanism.

Figure 4: Passkey retrieval accuracy of GSA and H-GSA on Qwen2-7B-Instruct (left two panels) and Llama3.2-1B (right two panels). The vertical axis denotes the context length, while the horizontal axis represents the relative insertion position (%) of the passkey.
Table 4: Performance comparison on RAG under different compression ratios (8×, 16×) using Llama3.2-1B. Best results are in bold, second-best are underlined.
Table 5: RAG benchmarks with KV-cache Reuse under different compression ratios (8×, 16×) using Llama3.2-1B. Best results are in bold.
Table 3: Performance comparison on LongBench under different compression ratios (8×, 16×, 32×) using Qwen2-7B-Instruct. Best results are in bold, second-best are underlined.

Figure 2: (a) Comparison of trainable sparse attention methods (left) and GSA pipeline (right). Left: NSA replaces standard attention with three parallel architectural branches (compress, select, sliding window); DSA relies on an external lightning indexer to select tokens before sparse attention; MoBA uses non-differentiable mean pooling over blocks as routing gates. Right: GSA operates in four stages within the standard Transformer: (1) interleaved gist tokens are trained to compress each chunk via a causal mask; (2) the query scores all gist tokens and selects the top-k most relevant (starred); (3) the selected chunk is unfolded by restoring its raw tokens alongside its gist token (red dashed box); (4) sparse attention is computed over the selected gist-chunk pairs only. Unlike NSA, DSA, and MoBA, GSA requires no architectural modifications, external modules, or nondifferentiable operations. (b) The gist causal mask Mgist for a sequence with chunk size L = 2. Rows represent queries; columns represent keys. Dark cells indicate allowed attention. (c) Attention patterns with a two-level gist hierarchy. The grayed-out region represents compressed history accessible only via the meta-gist. Raw and gist tokens (light/dark blue) attend to local context and preceding meta-gists; and the meta-gist (red) attends to its child gists and the preceding meta-gists.
